title: Google and Thinking Machines Lab ink $10B+ AI infrastructure deal
summary: Google Cloud has signed a multi-billion-dollar agreement with Mira Murati's Thinking Machines Lab for AI infrastructure using Nvidia's GB300 chips. This deal underscores the growing collaboration between tech giants and independent AI labs.
body:
Google Cloud has entered into a multi-billion-dollar partnership with Mira Murati's Thinking Machines Lab, according to an exclusive report by TechCrunch AI. The deal will provide the lab with AI infrastructure powered by Nvidia's latest GB300 chips, marking a significant investment in advanced AI capabilities. This collaboration highlights the increasing importance of specialized AI labs in driving innovation.

The partnership is notable for several reasons. First, it underscores the strategic importance of Nvidia's GB300 chips, which are designed to handle the most demanding AI workloads. Second, it highlights the growing trend of tech giants like Google partnering with independent AI labs to stay at the forefront of AI development. This deal could set a new standard for similar collaborations in the industry.

Looking ahead, this deal is likely to spur further investments in AI infrastructure and chip technology. It also raises questions about the future of AI development, particularly how independent labs and tech giants will collaborate to push the boundaries of what's possible. The deal's success could influence other major players to follow suit, potentially leading to a new wave of AI advancements.
twitterThread:
🚨 Google just dropped a $10B+ bomb on AI.

They're partnering with Mira Murati's Thinking Machines Lab for Nvidia GB300-powered infrastructure. This is next-level.

Here's why this matters:

1. Nvidia's GB300 chips are the gold standard for AI workloads. Google is betting big on them.

2. This isn't just a deal—it's a strategic alliance. Google wants Thinking Machines' expertise.

3. The AI lab arms race just got real.

The wild part? This deal could redefine how tech giants and independent labs collaborate.

Imagine every major player rushing to secure their own AI lab partnerships.

This changes everything for AI startups.

Google isn't just investing in chips. They're investing in the future of AI.

This deal could accelerate breakthroughs in AI research and deployment.

Let that sink in.

Hot take: We're about to see a wave of similar deals. Every major cloud provider will want their own AI lab partnership.

The AI infrastructure game just got a lot more competitive.
